sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root כָּתַשׁ).-

(1) a mortar, Pro 27:22.

(2) Jud. 15:19 , prob. mortariolum dentium, Gr. ὁλμίσκος, socket of a tooth, see Bochart, Hieroz. t. i. p. 202. [Is it not a place of such a form that is spoken of in the cited passage?]

(3) Zep 1:11 [Maktesh], pr.n. of a valley near Jerusalem, prob. so called from its resemblance to a mortar.
